mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and consumer credit outstanding reflects a synchronized withdrawal of household balance sheet leverage, which compresses aggregate demand and forces equity multiples lower over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 1.5% over a single-series baseline, or forward returns do not differ significantly between high and low interaction quartiles.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained industrial production growth above trend, capacity utilization constraints drive input cost inflation, which erodes corporate margins and suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average by more than 2%, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth accelerates corporate revenue expectations, which directly lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The cross-sectional correlation between GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.10 over any 3-year period, or returns are flat across growth deciles.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, borrowing costs cross a critical threshold that compresses equity risk premiums and forces a repricing of long-duration assets, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive in > 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in unemployment and decline in nonfarm payrolls confirms a structural labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines in out-of-sample backtests, or forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when both proxies move contrarily.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, real GDP growth becomes a negative signal for equities as it reflects overheating that invites aggressive monetary tightening, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when real GDP growth accelerates alongside CPI above 3.0%, or the regime-specific predictive power vanishes in rolling 18-month windows.
